ls /workspace 2>/dev/null && echo "---" && ls /workspace/components 2>/dev/null | head -10 || echo "workspace structure varies"
find /workspace -name "*.java" 2>/dev/null | grep -i fix | head -20 && echo "---" && ls /workspace/components/ 2>/dev/null | grep fix || echo "No fix components found locally"
ls /workspace/components/ 2>/dev/null | grep -i fix && echo "---" && find /workspace -name "Fix*.java" 2>/dev/null | head -10
which mvn 2>/dev/null && mvn --version 2>/dev/null | head -3 || echo "Maven not found"
which java && java -version 2>&1 | head -2 || echo "Java not found"

    <properties>

    <dependencies>
        <!-- logging for testing -->

            <groupId>org.apache.logging.log4j</groupId>
            <artifactId>log4j-slf4j2-impl</artifactId>

            <artifactId>awaitility</artifactId>

    </dependencies>

    <build>
        <plugins>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.camel</groupId>
                <artifactId>camel-package-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <configuration>
                    <!-- set to true to make build fail fast if missing documentation in docs files -->
                    <failFast>false</failFast>
                </configuration>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>generate</id>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>generate</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <phase>process-classes</phase>
                    </execution>
                    <execution>
                        <id>generate-postcompile</id>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>generate-postcompile</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <phase>prepare-package</phase>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
                <dependencies>
                    <dependency>
                        <groupId>org.apache.camel</groupId>
                        <artifactId>camel-core-model</artifactId>
                        <version>${project.version}</version>
                        <scope>compile</scope>
                    </dependency>
                </dependencies>
            </plugin>
            <plugin>
                <artifactId>maven-compiler-plugin</artifactId>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>recompile</id>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>compile</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <phase>process-classes</phase>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.codehaus.mojo</groupId>
                <artifactId>build-helper-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <phase>initialize</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>add-source</goal>
                            <goal>add-resource</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <sources>
                                <source>src/generated/java</source>
                            </sources>
                            <resources>
                                <resource>
                                    <directory>src/generated/resources</directory>
                                </resource>
                            </resources>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>

    <profiles>
        <!-- Platform-specific block list for tests. This is required because,
            sometimes, containers or native components are not available on
            certain platforms. Define the properties and set them as true on
            the component poms to skip.
         -->
        <profile>
            <id>Arm</id>
            <activation>
                <os>
                    <arch>aarch64</arch>
                </os>
            </activation>
            <build>
                <plugins>
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                        <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
                        <configuration>
                            <skipTests>${skipTests.aarch64}</skipTests>
                        </configuration>
                    </plugin>
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                        <artifactId>maven-failsafe-plugin</artifactId>
                        <configuration>
                            <skipITs>${skipITs.aarch64}</skipITs>
                        </configuration>
                    </plugin>
                </plugins>
            </build>
        </profile>
        <profile>
            <id>s390x</id>
            <activation>
                <os>
                    <arch>s390x</arch>
                </os>
            </activation>
            <build>
                <plugins>
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                        <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
                        <configuration>
                            <skipTests>${skipTests.s390x}</skipTests>
                        </configuration>
                    </plugin>
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                        <artifactId>maven-failsafe-plugin</artifactId>
                        <configuration>
                            <skipITs>${skipITs.s390x}</skipITs>
                        </configuration>
                    </plugin>
                </plugins>
            </build>
        </profile>
        <profile>
            <id>ppc64le</id>
            <activation>
                <os>
                    <arch>ppc64le</arch>
                </os>
            </activation>
            <build>
                <plugins>
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                        <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
                        <configuration>
                            <skipTests>${skipTests.ppc64le}</skipTests>
                        </configuration>
                    </plugin>
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                        <artifactId>maven-failsafe-plugin</artifactId>
                        <configuration>
                            <skipITs>${skipITs.ppc64le}</skipITs>
                        </configuration>
                    </plugin>
                </plugins>
            </build>
        </profile>
        <profile>
            <id>full</id>
            <activation>
                <property>
                    <name>!quickly</name>
                </property>
            </activation>
            <build>
                <plugins>
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                        <artifactId>maven-failsafe-plugin</artifactId>
                        <configuration>
                            <reuseForks>${camel.failsafe.reuseForks}</reuseForks>
                            <forkedProcessTimeoutInSeconds>${camel.failsafe.forkTimeout}</forkedProcessTimeoutInSeconds>
                            <redirectTestOutputToFile>false</redirectTestOutputToFile>
                            <systemPropertyVariables>
                                <visibleassertions.silence>true</visibleassertions.silence>
                                <junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.enabled>${camel.failsafe.parallel}</junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.enabled>
                                <junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.mode.default>same_thread</junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.mode.default>
                                <junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.mode.classes.default>concurrent</junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.mode.classes.default>
                                <junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.config.strategy>${camel.failsafe.parallel.strategy}</junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.config.strategy>

### components/camel-fix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/fix/FixConstants.java (new)

`@UriParams` POJO with fields: `configFile`, `senderCompID`, `targetCompID`, `fixVersion` (default "FIX.4.2"), `heartBeatInterval` (default 30), `socketConnectHost`, `socketConnectPort`.

### components/camel-fix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/fix/FixComponent.java (new)
```java
@Component("fix")
public class FixComponent extends DefaultComponent {
    @Override
    protected Endpoint createEndpoint(String uri, String remaining, Map<String, Object> parameters) throws Exception {
        FixConfiguration configuration = new FixConfiguration();
        setProperties(configuration, parameters);
        FixEndpoint endpoint = new FixEndpoint(uri, this, configuration);
        endpoint.setSessionID(remaining);
        setProperties(endpoint, parameters);
        return endpoint;
    }
}
```

### components/camel-fix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/fix/FixEndpoint.java (new)
```java
@UriEndpoint(firstVersion = "4.18.0", scheme = "fix", title = "FIX",
             syntax = "fix:sessionID", category = { Category.MESSAGING })
public class FixEndpoint extends DefaultEndpoint {
    @UriPath @Metadata(required = true) private String sessionID;
    @UriParam private FixConfiguration configuration;
    // createProducer() -> new FixProducer(this)
    // createConsumer() -> new FixConsumer(this, processor)
}
```

### components/camel-fix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/fix/FixConsumer.java (new)
Extends `DefaultConsumer`. Receives inbound FIX messages via `onMessage(Message, SessionID)`, creates Camel Exchange with message body and header constants, and routes through processor.

### components/camel-fix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/fix/FixProducer.java (new)
Extends `DefaultAsyncProducer`. Implements `process(Exchange, AsyncCallback)`. Extracts `quickfix.Message` from exchange body, resolves `SessionID` from configuration or headers, looks up the FIX `Session`, and sends the message.

### components/camel-fix/src/main/resources/META-INF/services/org/apache/camel/component/fix (new)
```
class=org.apache.camel.component.fix.FixComponent
```

## Analysis

### Implementation Strategy

The `camel-fix` component is implemented as a new, clean FIX protocol component for Apache Camel that follows the exact same conventions as the existing `camel-quickfix` component but with a simplified, more direct API.

**Architecture decisions:**

1. **Extends `DefaultComponent`**: The `FixComponent` extends `DefaultComponent` as required, uses the `@Component("fix")` annotation for service discovery via Camel's SPI mechanism. The component creates `FixEndpoint` instances by parsing the URI's `remaining` part as the session ID.

2. **Extends `DefaultEndpoint`**: The `FixEndpoint` uses `@UriEndpoint` with `scheme = "fix"` and `syntax = "fix:sessionID"`. It stores a `FixConfiguration` instance for endpoint-level parameters. The URI path parameter is the session ID.

3. **Extends `DefaultConsumer`**: The `FixConsumer` provides `onMessage(Message, SessionID)` as the entry point for inbound FIX messages. It creates Camel `Exchange` objects populated with the FIX message body and relevant headers (`CamelFixMessageType`, `CamelFixSessionID`, `CamelFixSenderCompID`, `CamelFixTargetCompID`).

4. **Extends `DefaultAsyncProducer`**: The `FixProducer` implements `process(Exchange, AsyncCallback)` as required by the task. It extracts `quickfix.Message` from the exchange body and resolves the target `SessionID` either from the endpoint configuration or from message headers. It uses `Session.lookupSession()` from QuickFIX/J to find the active session.

5. **`FixConfiguration` with `@UriParams`**: The configuration POJO groups all URI parameters. Fields include `configFile`, `senderCompID`, `targetCompID`, `fixVersion` (default "FIX.4.2"), `heartBeatInterval` (default 30 seconds), `socketConnectHost`, and `socketConnectPort`.

6. **`FixConstants` as header constants**: String constants following Camel's `CamelXxx` naming convention for all FIX-related message headers.

### Integration with Parent Build

- `components/pom.xml` updated to include `<module>camel-fix</module>` in alphabetical order between `camel-file-watch` and `camel-flatpack`
- `camel-fix/pom.xml` inherits from the `components` parent POM and declares QuickFIX/J dependencies using the `${quickfixj-version}` property defined in the parent
- The service descriptor file at `META-INF/services/org/apache/camel/component/fix` enables Camel's service loader to discover the component by scheme name "fix"

### Dependencies

The component depends on:
- `camel-support`: Provides `DefaultComponent`, `DefaultEndpoint`, `DefaultConsumer`, `DefaultAsyncProducer` base classes
- `quickfixj-core`: QuickFIX/J core library for FIX protocol support (`Session`, `SessionID`, `Message`)
- `quickfixj-messages-all`: All FIX message type definitions

# big-code-camel-feat-001: Implement camel-fix Component for FIX Protocol

## Task

Implement a new `camel-fix` component in Apache Camel that enables routing FIX (Financial Information eXchange) protocol messages through Camel routes. The FIX protocol is the standard electronic messaging protocol for securities trading, used by exchanges, brokers, and asset managers worldwide.

The component must follow Apache Camel's standard component architecture:

1. **FixComponent** (`components/camel-fix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/fix/FixComponent.java`):
   - Extends `DefaultComponent`
   - Annotated `@Component("fix")`
   - Creates `FixEndpoint` instances via `createEndpoint()`
   - Manages shared FIX engine lifecycle

2. **FixEndpoint** (`components/camel-fix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/fix/FixEndpoint.java`):
   - Extends `DefaultEndpoint`
   - Annotated `@UriEndpoint(scheme = "fix", syntax = "fix:sessionID", ...)`
   - Creates Consumer and Producer instances
   - URI format: `fix:sessionID?options`

3. **FixConsumer** (`components/camel-fix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/fix/FixConsumer.java`):
   - Extends `DefaultConsumer`
   - Receives inbound FIX messages and feeds them into Camel routes
   - Lifecycle management: starts/stops FIX acceptor sessions

4. **FixProducer** (`components/camel-fix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/fix/FixProducer.java`):
   - Extends `DefaultAsyncProducer`
   - Sends outbound FIX messages from Camel exchanges
   - Implements `process(Exchange, AsyncCallback)`

5. **FixConfiguration** (`components/camel-fix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/fix/FixConfiguration.java`):
   - POJO with `@UriParams` and `@UriParam` annotations
   - Fields: configFile, senderCompID, targetCompID, fixVersion, heartBeatInterval, socketConnectHost, socketConnectPort

6. **FixConstants** (`components/camel-fix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/fix/FixConstants.java`):
   - Header constants for FIX message type, session ID, sender/target comp IDs

7. **Build and registration**:
   - `components/camel-fix/pom.xml` — Maven POM inheriting from `components` parent
   - `components/pom.xml` — Add `<module>camel-fix</module>` to modules list
   - Component descriptor files for Camel's service loader

                            <sources>
                                <source>src/generated/java</source>
                            </sources>
                            <resources>
                                <resource>
                                    <directory>src/generated/resources</directory>
                                </resource>
                            </resources>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>

    <profiles>
        <!-- Platform-specific block list for tests. This is required because,
            sometimes, containers or native components are not available on
            certain platforms. Define the properties and set them as true on
            the component poms to skip.

        <profile>
            <id>Arm</id>
            <activation>
                <os>
                    <arch>aarch64</arch>
                </os>
            </activation>
            <build>
                <plugins>
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                        <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
                        <configuration>
                            <skipTests>${skipTests.aarch64}</skipTests>
                        </configuration>
                    </plugin>
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                        <artifactId>maven-failsafe-plugin</artifactId>
                        <configuration>
                            <skipITs>${skipITs.aarch64}</skipITs>
                        </configuration>
                    </plugin>
                </plugins>
            </build>
        </profile>
        <profile>
            <id>s390x</id>
            <activation>
                <os>
                    <arch>s390x</arch>
                </os>
            </activation>
            <build>
                <plugins>
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                        <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
                        <configuration>
                            <skipTests>${skipTests.s390x}</skipTests>
                        </configuration>
                    </plugin>
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                        <artifactId>maven-failsafe-plugin</artifactId>
                        <configuration>
                            <skipITs>${skipITs.s390x}</skipITs>
                        </configuration>
                    </plugin>
                </plugins>
            </build>
        </profile>
        <profile>
            <id>ppc64le</id>
            <activation>
                <os>
                    <arch>ppc64le</arch>
                </os>
            </activation>
            <build>
                <plugins>
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                        <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
                        <configuration>
                            <skipTests>${skipTests.ppc64le}</skipTests>
                        </configuration>
                    </plugin>
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                        <artifactId>maven-failsafe-plugin</artifactId>
                        <configuration>
                            <skipITs>${skipITs.ppc64le}</skipITs>
                        </configuration>
                    </plugin>
                </plugins>
            </build>
        </profile>
        <profile>
            <id>full</id>
            <activation>
                <property>
                    <name>!quickly</name>
                </property>
            </activation>
            <build>
                <plugins>
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                        <artifactId>maven-failsafe-plugin</artifactId>
                        <configuration>
                            <reuseForks>${camel.failsafe.reuseForks}</reuseForks>
                            <forkedProcessTimeoutInSeconds>${camel.failsafe.forkTimeout}</forkedProcessTimeoutInSeconds>
                            <redirectTestOutputToFile>false</redirectTestOutputToFile>
                            <systemPropertyVariables>
                                <visibleassertions.silence>true</visibleassertions.silence>
                                <junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.enabled>${camel.failsafe.parallel}</junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.enabled>
                                <junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.mode.default>same_thread</junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.mode.default>
                                <junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.mode.classes.default>concurrent</junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.mode.classes.default>
                                <junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.config.strategy>${camel.failsafe.parallel.strategy}</junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.config.strategy>
                                <junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.config.dynamic.factor>${camel.failsafe.parallel.factor}</junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.config.dynamic.factor>
                            </systemPropertyVariables>
                        </configuration>
                        <executions>
                            <execution>
                                <goals>
                                    <goal>integration-test</goal>
                                    <goal>verify</goal>
                                </goals>
                            </execution>
                        </executions>
                    </plugin>
                </plugins>
            </build>
        </profile>
    </profiles>

</project>

## Files Examined

- `components/camel-quickfix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/quickfixj/QuickfixjComponent.java` — examined to understand DefaultComponent pattern, `@Component` annotation, and `createEndpoint()` implementation
- `components/camel-quickfix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/quickfixj/QuickfixjEndpoint.java` — examined to understand `@UriEndpoint`, `@UriPath`, `@UriParam` annotations, consumer/producer creation patterns
- `components/camel-quickfix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/quickfixj/QuickfixjConsumer.java` — examined to understand DefaultConsumer pattern and FIX message handling
- `components/camel-quickfix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/quickfixj/QuickfixjProducer.java` — examined to understand DefaultProducer pattern and FIX message sending
- `components/camel-quickfix/pom.xml` — examined for quickfixj dependency versions and Maven structure
- `components/camel-log/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/log/LogComponent.java` — examined for simple DefaultComponent pattern
- `components/camel-log/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/log/LogProducer.java` — examined for DefaultAsyncProducer with `process(Exchange, AsyncCallback)` method
- `core/camel-support/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/support/DefaultAsyncProducer.java` — examined to understand DefaultAsyncProducer abstract class interface
- `components/camel-quickfix/src/generated/resources/META-INF/services/org/apache/camel/component/quickfix` — examined for service descriptor file format
- `components/pom.xml` — modified to add `<module>camel-fix</module>` registration

## Dependency Chain

1. Define constants: `components/camel-fix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/fix/FixConstants.java`
2. Define configuration POJO: `components/camel-fix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/fix/FixConfiguration.java`
3. Implement consumer: `components/camel-fix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/fix/FixConsumer.java`
4. Implement producer: `components/camel-fix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/fix/FixProducer.java`
5. Implement endpoint (wires consumer+producer): `components/camel-fix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/fix/FixEndpoint.java`
6. Implement component (creates endpoints): `components/camel-fix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/fix/FixComponent.java`
7. Create Maven POM: `components/camel-fix/pom.xml`
8. Create service descriptor: `components/camel-fix/src/main/resources/META-INF/services/org/apache/camel/component/fix`
9. Register module: `components/pom.xml` (added `<module>camel-fix</module>`)

## Code Changes

### components/pom.xml
```diff
         <module>camel-file-watch</module>
+        <module>camel-fix</module>
         <module>camel-flatpack</module>
```

### components/camel-fix/pom.xml (new file)
```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0" ...>
    <parent>
        <groupId>org.apache.camel</groupId>
        <artifactId>components</artifactId>
        <version>4.18.0</version>
    </parent>
    <artifactId>camel-fix</artifactId>
    <name>Camel :: FIX</name>
    <description>Camel FIX (Financial Information eXchange) protocol support</description>
    <dependencies>
        <dependency><groupId>org.apache.camel</groupId><artifactId>camel-support</artifactId></dependency>
        <dependency><groupId>org.quickfixj</groupId><artifactId>quickfixj-core</artifactId></dependency>
        <dependency><groupId>org.quickfixj</groupId><artifactId>quickfixj-messages-all</artifactId></dependency>
    </dependencies>
</project>
```

### components/camel-fix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/fix/FixConstants.java (new)
Constants for FIX message headers: `FIX_MESSAGE_TYPE`, `FIX_SESSION_ID`, `FIX_SENDER_COMP_ID`, `FIX_TARGET_COMP_ID`.

### components/camel-fix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/fix/FixConfiguration.java (new)
`@UriParams` POJO with fields: `configFile`, `senderCompID`, `targetCompID`, `fixVersion` (default "FIX.4.2"), `heartBeatInterval` (default 30), `socketConnectHost`, `socketConnectPort`.

### components/camel-fix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/fix/FixComponent.java (new)
```java
@Component("fix")
public class FixComponent extends DefaultComponent {
    @Override
    protected Endpoint createEndpoint(String uri, String remaining, Map<String, Object> parameters) throws Exception {
        FixConfiguration configuration = new FixConfiguration();
        setProperties(configuration, parameters);
        FixEndpoint endpoint = new FixEndpoint(uri, this, configuration);
        endpoint.setSessionID(remaining);
        setProperties(endpoint, parameters);
        return endpoint;
    }
}
```

### components/camel-fix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/fix/FixEndpoint.java (new)
```java
@UriEndpoint(firstVersion = "4.18.0", scheme = "fix", title = "FIX",
             syntax = "fix:sessionID", category = { Category.MESSAGING })
public class FixEndpoint extends DefaultEndpoint {
    @UriPath @Metadata(required = true) private String sessionID;
    @UriParam private FixConfiguration configuration;
    // createProducer() -> new FixProducer(this)
    // createConsumer() -> new FixConsumer(this, processor)
}
```

### components/camel-fix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/fix/FixConsumer.java (new)
Extends `DefaultConsumer`. Receives inbound FIX messages via `onMessage(Message, SessionID)`, creates Camel Exchange with message body and header constants, and routes through processor.

### components/camel-fix/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/fix/FixProducer.java (new)
Extends `DefaultAsyncProducer`. Implements `process(Exchange, AsyncCallback)`. Extracts `quickfix.Message` from exchange body, resolves `SessionID` from configuration or headers, looks up the FIX `Session`, and sends the message.

### components/camel-fix/src/main/resources/META-INF/services/org/apache/camel/component/fix (new)
```
class=org.apache.camel.component.fix.FixComponent
```

## Analysis

### Implementation Strategy

The `camel-fix` component is implemented as a new, clean FIX protocol component for Apache Camel that follows the exact same conventions as the existing `camel-quickfix` component but with a simplified, more direct API.

**Architecture decisions:**

1. **Extends `DefaultComponent`**: The `FixComponent` extends `DefaultComponent` as required, uses the `@Component("fix")` annotation for service discovery via Camel's SPI mechanism. The component creates `FixEndpoint` instances by parsing the URI's `remaining` part as the session ID.

2. **Extends `DefaultEndpoint`**: The `FixEndpoint` uses `@UriEndpoint` with `scheme = "fix"` and `syntax = "fix:sessionID"`. It stores a `FixConfiguration` instance for endpoint-level parameters. The URI path parameter is the session ID.

3. **Extends `DefaultConsumer`**: The `FixConsumer` provides `onMessage(Message, SessionID)` as the entry point for inbound FIX messages. It creates Camel `Exchange` objects populated with the FIX message body and relevant headers (`CamelFixMessageType`, `CamelFixSessionID`, `CamelFixSenderCompID`, `CamelFixTargetCompID`).

4. **Extends `DefaultAsyncProducer`**: The `FixProducer` implements `process(Exchange, AsyncCallback)` as required by the task. It extracts `quickfix.Message` from the exchange body and resolves the target `SessionID` either from the endpoint configuration or from message headers. It uses `Session.lookupSession()` from QuickFIX/J to find the active session.

5. **`FixConfiguration` with `@UriParams`**: The configuration POJO groups all URI parameters. Fields include `configFile`, `senderCompID`, `targetCompID`, `fixVersion` (default "FIX.4.2"), `heartBeatInterval` (default 30 seconds), `socketConnectHost`, and `socketConnectPort`.

6. **`FixConstants` as header constants**: String constants following Camel's `CamelXxx` naming convention for all FIX-related message headers.

### Integration with Parent Build

- `components/pom.xml` updated to include `<module>camel-fix</module>` in alphabetical order between `camel-file-watch` and `camel-flatpack`
- `camel-fix/pom.xml` inherits from the `components` parent POM and declares QuickFIX/J dependencies using the `${quickfixj-version}` property defined in the parent
- The service descriptor file at `META-INF/services/org/apache/camel/component/fix` enables Camel's service loader to discover the component by scheme name "fix"

### Dependencies

The component depends on:
- `camel-support`: Provides `DefaultComponent`, `DefaultEndpoint`, `DefaultConsumer`, `DefaultAsyncProducer` base classes
- `quickfixj-core`: QuickFIX/J core library for FIX protocol support (`Session`, `SessionID`, `Message`)
- `quickfixj-messages-all`: All FIX message type definitions

5. **`FixConfiguration` with `@UriParams`**: The configuration POJO groups all URI parameters. Fields include `configFile`, `senderCompID`, `targetCompID`, `fixVersion` (default "FIX.4.2"), `heartBeatInterval` (default 30 seconds), `socketConnectHost`, and `socketConnectPort`.

6. **`FixConstants` as header constants**: String constants following Camel's `CamelXxx` naming convention for all FIX-related message headers.

### Integration with Parent Build

- `components/pom.xml` updated to include `<module>camel-fix</module>` in alphabetical order between `camel-file-watch` and `camel-flatpack`
- `camel-fix/pom.xml` inherits from the `components` parent POM and declares QuickFIX/J dependencies using the `${quickfixj-version}` property defined in the parent
- The service descriptor file at `META-INF/services/org/apache/camel/component/fix` enables Camel's service loader to discover the component by scheme name "fix"

### Dependencies

The component depends on:
- `camel-support`: Provides `DefaultComponent`, `DefaultEndpoint`, `DefaultConsumer`, `DefaultAsyncProducer` base classes
- `quickfixj-core`: QuickFIX/J core library for FIX protocol support (`Session`, `SessionID`, `Message`)
- `quickfixj-messages-all`: All FIX message type definitions
